channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Competitive Programming Series — Session 2: Recursion and Backtracking
item.description: After covering the foundational building blocks in Session 1, the next step is one of the most important problem-solving techniques in all of programming: recursion. And once recursion feels...

item.title: DuckDB Data Inlining, SQLite Fossildelta OOB, Postgres 19 Temporal Data
item.description: DuckDB Data Inlining, SQLite Fossildelta OOB, Postgres 19 Temporal Data Today's Highlights Today's highlights include DuckDB's innovative data inlining for stream processing in data lakes,...

item.description: AI Fluency for Software Engineers: A Practical Playbook Beyond Prompting A few years ago, being productive with AI mostly meant knowing which tool to open and what question to ask. Today, that is...

item.description: Competitive programming often looks like a race to write code as fast as possible. But the real secret is simpler: the best competitive programmers are not just faster typists — they are better at...

item.title: Zapier vs Make vs n8n 2026: The Honest Comparison (Including the Free Option)
item.description: Verdict: Quick verdict: Zapier wins on simplicity and breadth — 7,000+ integrations, no-code setup, great for non-technical users. Make (formerly Integromat) wins on power-per-dollar — complex...

item.title: ‘Tell Him He’s a Piece of Shit’: Meta’s New AI Unit Is a Total Mess
item.description: Executives and employees alike are struggling with Meta's chaotic AI strategy, according to sources and internal discussions reviewed by WIRED.

item.title: The First Message Sent Over the Internet Was 'LO'
item.description: The first message ever sent across the network that became the internet was not "Hello, world." It was not a grand declaration. It was two letters, transmitted by accident, before the system fell...

item.title: What We Learned Scanning Netflix Atlas
item.description: Clear Code Intelligence scanned a public Netflix repository: Netflix/atlas. This is not a dunk on Netflix. It is a public-code methodology test. After scanning Google zx and Microsoft...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid-Inspired Fluid Pump
item.description: This fluid pump was inspired by the way squids propel themselves through the water. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t...

item.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims’ sued by Google
item.description: The tech giant said a group called "Outsider Enterprise" used AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims, sending 2.5 million text messages over a span of two weeks.

item.title: SpaceX IPO: Live updates on everything you need to know
item.description: TechCrunch has followed SpaceX's start, struggles, and successes from the early days. And we're here for what happens next too. This package of SpaceX IPO coverage includes who stands to win (and...

item.title: Nothing CEO says phone prices are going to keep going up
item.description: If you're thinking about upgrading your phone, "the best time was yesterday," according to Nothing CEO and co-founder Carl Pei, echoing a message we heard during MWC. As Android Authority reports,...

item.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
item.description: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware...

item.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
item.description: Vulnerability in the Oracle-owned PeopleSoft software is about as critical as they come.

item.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
item.description: Google on Friday said it's pursuing legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network, accusing it of using its Gemini artificial intelligence (AI) agent to send phishing text messages targeting...

item.title: The world’s first trillionaire is a killer
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO will probably make him the richest person to ever walk the planet. And while his mountain of horrible personal conduct could fill multiple books, one fact in particular...

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade
item.description: Instead of hiding on the laptops and servers defenders watch most closely, a China-nexus group spent close to a decade hidden inside the Linux login system itself. Sygnia, which tracks the group...

item.title: Mistral is rumored to be raising €3B at €20B valuation
item.description: The funding round would value the company at around €20 billion (about $23.15 billion), nearly double its Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

item.title: China Didn’t Make Americans Hate Data Centers
item.description: GOP lawmakers, tech investors, and even OpenAI have tied the anti-data-center movement in the US to Chinese interference. Experts say it’s much more complicated than that.

item.title: Siri is good now??
item.description: You'd be forgiven for thinking this day would never come. Siri has spent a decade and half somewhere between "sort of useful at a few things" and "utterly disastrous, why did I even try, can it...

item.title: A trillion dollars is a stupid amount of money
item.description: Elon Musk is now officially the world's first trillionaire. That is a colossal amount of wealth (and by proxy, power) for one individual to have. Its scale - a thousand times more than a billion -...

item.title: Elon Musk is the world’s first trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk's net worth has passed the trillion-dollar mark after SpaceX's IPO. His net worth, which was hovering around $800 billion before the IPO, includes the value of his 4.8 billion shares in...

item.title: SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI’s hot IPO summer
item.description: The IPO market is back, and it’s not the same companies leading the charge. FAANG had a good run, but a new acronym is taking over: MANGOS — Meta (or Microsoft, depending on who you ask),...

item.title: I held the Trump phone
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. We've reached out, as usual, to ask about the Trump phone's whereabouts. We don't have the phones we preordered yet, but...

item.title: olmo-eval: An evaluation workbench for the model development loop

item.title: Govee’s rechargeable smart table lamp is down to $60
item.description: Whether you’re planning a backyard barbeque or a World Cup watch party, Govee’s Table Lamp Classic can help set the mood with color-changing lighting effects. Right now it’s down to just $59.99...
